member.name: Alex Burghart
member.synopsis: Alex Burghart is the Conservative MP for Brentwood and Ongar , and has been an MP continually since 8 June 2017. He currently undertakes the roles of Shadow Chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster, and Shadow Secretary of State for Northern Ireland.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Lobbying
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what is the status of the Cabinet Office guidance from 2010 entitled Rules on Lobbying for Non-Departmental Public Bodies.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Honorable Member to the answer provided to UIN 116156 on 6 March 2026, which confirmed that the principles of the Rules on lobbying for non-departmental public bodies (NDPBs) guidance, published in October 2010, remain Government policy.

writtenQuestions.heading: Lord Mandelson
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, pursuant to the Answer of 12 June 2026 to Question 6444 on Lord Mandelson, if she will set out in which ways the policy of commenting on individual personnel matters has appl
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government's policy of not commenting on individual personnel matters remains unchanged, but where the Humble Address process required the disclosure of relevant documentation, we have met those requirements.

writtenQuestions.heading: Overseas Students: EU Nationals
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether the Government plans to offer EU students home tuition fee rates in order to facilitate a new EU/UK agreement.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Most new EU students who were not lawfully resident in the UK before the end of the transition period on 31 December 2020 pay international tuition fees. Changes to tuition fees status for EU students were not agreed at the May 2025 Summit and do not form...

writtenQuestions.heading: Lord Mandelson
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, with reference to the Answer of 2 December 2025 to Question 94015 on Lord Mandelson and Deposited Paper 2025-0825, committed on 2 December 2025, if he will publish the information provided in answer to Questions
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the Government’s statement and release of information on 1st June, providing an update on the response to the Humble Address. The documents published in the first and second tranches contain the entirety of the documents the Gove...

writtenQuestions.heading: Gordon Brown
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, pursuant to the answer of 29 June 2026 to Question 10647 on Gordon Brown, whether his office is in (a) 70 Whitehall, (b) Dover House and (c) 26 Whitehall.
writtenQuestions.answerText: For security reasons we do not comment on the specific location of individual’s offices. The Prime Minister’s Special Reviewer on Global Finance and Cooperation has access to appropriate office space within the Cabinet Office’s existing footprint if requi...

writtenQuestions.heading: Anthony Finkelstein
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, with reference to the news story entitled Independent Review of the Use of Non-corporate Communications Channels in Government, of 2 July 2026, whether Professor Sir Anthony Finkelstein is a direct Ministerial a
writtenQuestions.answerText: Professor Sir Anthony Finkelstein’s appointment as the Independent Reviewer of the Use of Non-corporate Communications Channels is a Tier 1 Direct Ministerial Appointment made by the Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister and Chancellor of the Duchy of Lan...

writtenQuestions.heading: Ministers: Travel
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether the Cabinet Office Propriety and Ethics Team has issued guidance on whether Ministers can claim a proportion of their travel and accommodation costs in relation to family holidays overseas if they conduc
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministerial Code sets out principles relating to ministerial travel for official business. Section 10.1 of the Ministerial Code states that “Ministers must ensure that they always make efficient and cost‑effective travel arrangements. Official transpo...

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office: Public Expenditure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, With reference to the answer of 16 April 2026, to Question 124809, on FCDO: Inter Mediate, and with reference to the publication, FCDO: departmental spending over £25,000, fo
writtenQuestions.answerText: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) spending transparency data for the period since January 2025 has been delayed as a result of the transition to a new reporting process, but will be published in the normal way in due course.Such delays a...

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office: Marketing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, pursuant to the answer of 4 June 2026 to Question 4161 on Government departments: marketing, how much has been spent on branded goods and merchandise by her Department since
writtenQuestions.answerText: The requested data is not centrally held by the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO), and could only be collated and verified for the purposes of answering this question at disproportionate cost.

writtenQuestions.heading: China: Treaties
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, whether any (a) Memorandums of Understanding and (b) non-legally binding agreements have been agreed between the (i) department and (ii) its agencies, and with the People’s R
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer provided on 16 April in response to Question 126612.

writtenQuestions.heading: Lord Mandelson
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, pursuant to the Answer of 25 June 2026 to Question 428 on Members: Correspondence, and with reference to the FCDO Complaints Procedure published on gov.uk, whether a formal c
writtenQuestions.answerText: Stage one of the Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office (FCDO) Complaints Procedure published on gov.uk relates specifically to complaints about consular service. This would not be an appropriate avenue for a complaint of this nature to be pursued.

writtenQuestions.heading: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office: Government Communication Service
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, with reference to the answer of 5 June 2026 to Question 3763 on Government Communications Service: Staff, and of 19 January 2026 to Question 104195 on Government Communicatio
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon Member to the answer of 5 June from the Cabinet Office cited in his question.

writtenQuestions.heading: Bank Notes: Design
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if she will publish a copy of the full focus group findings referenced in Bank of England's Banknote imagery advisory group minutes - October 2025, meeting held on 14 October 2025, Item 3, published on 17 April 2026
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Bank of England is operationally independent in its banknote production decisions. The Bank will respond to the honorable Member, and a copy of the letter will be placed in the Library of the House of Commons.

writtenQuestions.heading: Bank of England: Connect
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, how much has the Bank of England spent on public affairs services provided by Connect since July 2024, and for what purposes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This is a matter for the operationally independent Bank of England. The Bank will respond to the honorable Member, and a copy of the letter will be placed in the Library of the House of Commons.

writtenQuestions.heading: Cabinet Office: Civil Servants
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, pursuant to the answer of 1 July 2026, to Question 12790, on Civil Service: Dual Nationals, and to the answer of 30 June 2026, to Question 13160, on Cabinet office: Civil Servants, for what reasons the posts in
writtenQuestions.answerText: The majority of posts in the EU Relations Secretariat are not in the categories capable of being reserved for UK nationals, as specified in Section 3 of the Civil Service Nationality Rules.

writtenQuestions.heading: Lord Mandelson
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, Further to the answer of 30 June 2026, to Question HL1329, on Lord Mandelson, whether any former civil servant or former Minister who was requested to provide information declined to do so.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is confident that all relevant departments and individuals were engaged through a comprehensive commissioning process managed by the Cabinet Office. All departmental Permanent Secretaries were required to provide assurance that they had tak...

writtenQuestions.heading: Bank Notes: Design
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, what is the policy of the Office for Equality and Opportunity on the Bank of England’s proposals to remove historic figures from bank notes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The design of bank notes is a matter for the Bank of England, which is independent of the Government.

writtenQuestions.heading: Bank Notes: Design
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for Women and Equalities, pursuant to the answer of 25 June 2026, to Question 10697, on Bank Notes: Design, what discussions the Office for Equality and Opportunity had with the Bank of England on the proposed changes to bank note desi
writtenQuestions.answerText: The design of bank notes is a matter for the Bank of England, which is independent of the Government. There has been no engagement between Cabinet Office or Equalities Ministers and the Bank of England on banknote design and imagery. Officials in the Off...

writtenQuestions.heading: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology: Public Appointments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether her Department's GDPR guidelines allow the publication of declared political activity for their Direct Ministerial Appointments.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the Cabinet Office 'Guidance on Making Direct Ministerial Appointments', these are appointments made directly by ministers which are also public office holders, but which are not public appointments. The Department publishes Direct Ministeri...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Service: Labour Turnover
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what assessment has (1) his Department and (2) Civil Service People Group made of the (i) level and (ii) frequency of churn in Civil Service positions, in relation to (A) staff moving positions within department
writtenQuestions.answerText: Assessments of turnover and time in post are made routinely on an annual basis. The available figures for each point are:A - Figures on movements within departments for the Civil Service as a whole are not available centrally, however estimates for the ce...
